messages.content: which of the following is the rate limiting enzyme of cholesterol synthesis?
which of the following is true regarding the regulation of the rate limiting enzyme in cholesterol synthesis?

The rate-limiting enzyme HMG-CoA reductase in cholesterol synthesis is tightly regulated through feedback mechanisms involving intracellular levels of cholesterol and other sterols. When cellular cholesterol levels are high, these molecules act as negative allosteric effectors to inhibit the enzyme, thus reducing the further synthesis of cholesterol. Conversely, when cholesterol levels are low, the enzyme is activated to increase the synthesis of cholesterol. Additionally, transcriptional regulation via the sterol regulatory element-binding proteins (SREBPs) can also modulate the expression of HMG-CoA reductase in response to cellular cholesterol levels.
which of the following is true regarding chylomicrons?

Chylomicrons are lipoprotein particles that transport dietary fats (triglycerides) from the intestine to various tissues in the body. They are synthesized in the intestinal mucosal cells and are composed of proteins, triglycerides, cholesterol, and phospholipids. Chylomicrons are the largest and least dense of all lipoproteins and play a key role in the absorption and transport of dietary lipids throughout the body. After being secreted into the lymphatic system, chylomicrons eventually enter the bloodstream, where they are broken down by lipoprotein lipase (LPL) to release their triglyceride content for energy utilization by tissues or storage in adipose tissue.

which of the following is true regarding chylomicrons?

nascent chylomicron carry apo-CII

mature chylomicron carry apo E only

remnant chylomicron carry apo-B48 and apocII

remnant chylomicron carry apo-B48 and apoE
remnant chylomicron carry apo-B48 and apoE

on macrophages can bind and  mediate the endocytosis of chemically modified LDL in which the lipid  components or apo B have been oxidized.

Scavenger receptors, such as scavenger receptor class A (SR-A) and CD36, on macrophages can bind and mediate the endocytosis of chemically modified low-density lipoprotein (LDL), in which the lipid components or apolipoprotein B (apoB) have been oxidized.
When cholesterol is taken up by HDL, it is immediately  esterified  by  the  plasma  enzyme -

The plasma enzyme that esterifies cholesterol when taken up by HDL is lecithin-cholesterol acyltransferase (LCAT). LCAT transfers a fatty acid from the sn-2 position of phosphatidylcholine to the hydroxyl group of cholesterol, converting it into cholesteryl ester.

After the cholesterol is accepted by HDL3, it is then esterified by LCAT,  increasing the size of the particles to form the less dense HDL2. 

True

False

Expression of the gene for HMG CoA reductase is inhibited by high cholesterol, as a result of which, de novo cholesterol synthesis decreases.

True

False

False. High cholesterol levels lead to the downregulation of HMG-CoA reductase activity, not the expression of the gene. This downregulation reduces the conversion of HMG-CoA to mevalonate, which decreases cholesterol synthesis.
